entries.name: Inbox
entries.description: Handles L1-to-L2 messaging. Users deposit funds or send messages here, which are organized into a Merkle tree for the sequencer to include in an L2 block.

entries.name: Rollup
entries.description: Core rollup logic contract. It processes block proposals, verifies ZK proofs for state transitions, manages data availability, and coordinates validator selection and chain tip updates.

getManaLimit.description: gas limit, currently 0 to prevent transactions

getEntryQueueFlushSize.description: number of sequencers flushable per epoch: will move from the entry queue to the active validator set if flushed

entries.name: RewardBooster
entries.description: Calculates 'boosted' reward shares for active provers based on a configured curve, incentivizing consistent participation.

entries.name: AlphaPayload
entries.description: The Alpha Upgrade Governance Payload encoding the recipe for bringing execution to the next Aztec rollup smart contract system.

entries.name: TallySlashingProposer
entries.description: Allows the validator committee to vote on slashing a specific peer. Once a quorum is reached, it proposes a slash action to the Slasher.

entries.name: FlushRewarder
entries.description: Incentivizes 'flushing' of the sequencer join queue. Flushing is needed to add sequencers to the active set at a preset rate limit and incurs gas costs. Adding one sequencer is rewarded by 100 AZTEC as long as supply lasts.

entries.name: SlashPayloadCloneable
entries.description: A template for specific slashing payload contracts deployed deterministically to encode a slashing action (who to slash and how much) that the Slasher executes.

entries.name: Slasher
entries.description: The executor contract for penalties. It receives authorization to slash validator stakes, subject to an execution delay and a vetoer.

entries.name: FeeJuicePortal
entries.description: A one-way public bridge to deposit AZTEC tokens to the Rollup.

entries.name: Outbox
entries.description: Handles L2-to-L1 messaging. It stores Merkle roots of messages exiting the rollup, allowing users to prove inclusion and finalize withdrawals or actions on L1.

entries.name: FlushRewarder
entries.description: Incentivizes 'flushing' of the sequencer join queue. Flushing is needed to add sequencers to the active set at a preset rate limit and incurs gas costs. Adding one sequencer is rewarded by 100 AZTEC as long as supply lasts.
